name: Tirupati Balaji Temple
shortDescription: One of the world's most visited pilgrimage sites dedicated to Lord Venkateswara.
description: Perched atop the Tirumala hills, this ancient temple draws millions of devotees every year for its rich rituals and grandeur.

name: Araku Valley
shortDescription: A scenic hill station known for coffee plantations and tribal culture.
description: Surrounded by lush valleys and waterfalls, Araku offers a refreshing escape with its coffee estates and tribal museum.

name: Visakhapatnam Beach
shortDescription: A popular coastal stretch known as the Jewel of the East Coast.
description: The beach city blends golden sands, naval heritage and hilltop viewpoints along the Bay of Bengal.

name: Borra Caves
shortDescription: Ancient limestone caves with stunning stalactite and stalagmite formations.
description: Formed over a million years, these caves offer a dramatic underground landscape amid the Eastern Ghats.

name: Tawang Monastery
shortDescription: India's largest Buddhist monastery set amid dramatic Himalayan scenery.
description: Founded in the 17th century, this monastery is a spiritual and cultural hub for the region's Monpa community.

name: Sela Pass
shortDescription: A high altitude mountain pass with a pristine glacial lake.
description: Connecting Tawang to the rest of Arunachal, this snow clad pass offers breathtaking Himalayan views.

name: Ziro Valley
shortDescription: A picturesque valley famous for Apatani culture and rice fields.
description: This UNESCO tentative site charms visitors with terraced paddies, pine hills and vibrant tribal traditions.

name: Namdapha National Park
shortDescription: A biodiversity rich rainforest park home to rare wildlife.
description: One of the largest protected areas in the country, it shelters tigers, clouded leopards and diverse flora.

name: Kaziranga National Park
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage site famous for the one horned rhinoceros.
description: Sprawling grasslands and wetlands make this park one of Asia's premier wildlife destinations.

name: Kamakhya Temple
shortDescription: A revered Shakti Peetha temple atop Nilachal Hill.
description: This ancient temple is one of the most important pilgrimage sites for Tantric worship in India.

name: Majuli Island
shortDescription: The world's largest river island known for Vaishnavite culture.
description: Dotted with satras and rustic villages, Majuli offers a serene glimpse into Assamese heritage.

name: Sivasagar
shortDescription: Historic seat of the Ahom kingdom with grand monuments.
description: The town is home to majestic tanks, palaces and temples built during six centuries of Ahom rule.

name: Mahabodhi Temple
shortDescription: The sacred site where Buddha attained enlightenment.
description: This UNESCO World Heritage temple complex is one of Buddhism's holiest pilgrimage destinations.

name: Nalanda Ruins
shortDescription: Remnants of one of the world's oldest universities.
description: These ancient ruins reveal the grandeur of a renowned center of learning dating back to the 5th century.

name: Patna Sahib
shortDescription: A significant Sikh gurudwara marking the birthplace of Guru Gobind Singh.
description: This elegant white gurudwara stands on the banks of the Ganges as a major pilgrimage site.

name: Vaishali
shortDescription: An ancient city linked to Buddhist and Jain heritage.
description: Known as one of the world's earliest republics, Vaishali holds relics and stupas of great historical value.

name: Chitrakoot Falls
shortDescription: Often called the Niagara of India for its horseshoe shaped cascade.
description: This wide waterfall on the Indravati River is especially spectacular during the monsoon season.

name: Bastar
shortDescription: A tribal heartland known for handicrafts and forests.
description: Bastar's dense forests and vibrant tribal festivals offer an immersive cultural experience.

name: Sirpur
shortDescription: An archaeological site with ancient Buddhist and Hindu monuments.
description: Excavations here have revealed temples, monasteries and artifacts dating back over a thousand years.

name: Kanger Valley National Park
shortDescription: A biodiverse park known for caves, waterfalls and rare wildlife.
description: The park's limestone caves and dense forests make it a unique destination in central India.

name: Baga Beach
shortDescription: A lively beach known for water sports and nightlife.
description: Baga is one of Goa's most popular stretches, packed with shacks, markets and beachside parties.

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
shortDescription: A UNESCO listed church housing the relics of St. Francis Xavier.
description: This baroque basilica is one of the finest examples of colonial Portuguese architecture in India.

name: Dudhsagar Falls
shortDescription: A majestic four tiered waterfall on the Goa Karnataka border.
description: Cascading through dense forest, this milky white waterfall is among India's tallest.

name: Fort Aguada
shortDescription: A 17th century Portuguese fort overlooking the Arabian Sea.
description: The fort's lighthouse and bastions offer panoramic views of the coastline and river confluence.

name: Rann of Kutch
shortDescription: A vast white salt desert famous for its annual Rann Utsav.
description: This surreal landscape transforms into a cultural festival ground during the winter months.

name: Statue of Unity
shortDescription: The world's tallest statue, honoring Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.
description: Standing at 182 meters, this monument offers a viewing gallery and museum on the Narmada riverbank.

name: Gir National Park
shortDescription: The only home of the Asiatic lion in the wild.
description: This dry deciduous forest is a vital conservation area for lions and diverse wildlife.

name: Somnath Temple
shortDescription: One of the twelve sacred Jyotirlinga shrines of Lord Shiva.
description: Rebuilt several times through history, this coastal temple holds deep religious significance.

name: Kurukshetra
shortDescription: The legendary battlefield of the Mahabharata and site of the Gita's teachings.
description: This holy city features sacred tanks and temples tied to ancient Indian epics.

name: Sultanpur National Park
shortDescription: A bird sanctuary popular among migratory species and birdwatchers.
description: The wetland park attracts flamingos, cranes and numerous native and migratory birds each winter.

name: Pinjore Gardens
shortDescription: A Mughal style terraced garden with fountains and pavilions.
description: Also known as Yadavindra Gardens, it showcases classic Mughal landscaping and architecture.

name: Morni Hills
shortDescription: Haryana's only hill station, offering lakes and scenic trails.
description: The forested hills provide a peaceful retreat with trekking trails and a serene lake.

name: Shimla Ridge
shortDescription: A colonial era promenade at the heart of Shimla town.
description: Lined with heritage buildings, the Ridge offers sweeping views of the surrounding Himalayan peaks.

name: Manali
shortDescription: A popular hill town gateway to adventure sports and valleys.
description: Surrounded by snow capped peaks, Manali is renowned for trekking, skiing and scenic beauty.

name: Rohtang Pass
shortDescription: A high mountain pass offering dramatic snow covered landscapes.
description: This scenic pass connects the Kullu Valley to Lahaul and Spiti, drawing adventure travelers.

name: Dharamshala
shortDescription: Home to the Dalai Lama and a hub of Tibetan culture.
description: Set against pine forests, this town blends Buddhist monasteries with colonial charm.

name: Betla National Park
shortDescription: A tiger reserve known for its rich forest wildlife.
description: The park is home to elephants, tigers and ancient forts within its dense sal forests.

name: Hundru Falls
shortDescription: A dramatic waterfall cascading from the Subarnarekha River.
description: Dropping from a height of over 300 feet, it is one of Jharkhand's most scenic spots.

name: Deoghar
shortDescription: Home to the sacred Baidyanath Jyotirlinga temple.
description: This pilgrim town draws devotees to its ancient Shiva shrine throughout the year.

name: Netarhat
shortDescription: A hill station known as the Queen of Chotanagpur.
description: Famous for its sunrise and sunset points, Netarhat offers cool weather and scenic plateaus.

name: Mysore Palace
shortDescription: A grand royal palace known for its Indo Saracenic architecture.
description: Illuminated with thousands of lights during Dasara, this palace is a symbol of Mysuru's royal legacy.

name: Hampi
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage site with ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire.
description: Scattered boulders and ancient temples make Hampi an open air museum of South Indian history.

name: Coorg
shortDescription: A misty hill region known as the Scotland of India.
description: Coorg's coffee plantations, waterfalls and rolling hills make it a favorite nature getaway.

name: Gokarna Beach
shortDescription: A tranquil coastal town with pristine beaches and temples.
description: Less crowded than Goa, Gokarna offers a laid back vibe alongside spiritual significance.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
shortDescription: Tranquil canals and lagoons famous for houseboat cruises.
description: Known as the Venice of the East, Alleppey offers a relaxing journey through palm fringed waterways.

name: Munnar
shortDescription: A hill station carpeted with sprawling tea plantations.
description: Rolling green hills and misty valleys make Munnar one of Kerala's most scenic destinations.

name: Fort Kochi
shortDescription: A historic coastal town blending Dutch, Portuguese and British influences.
description: Known for Chinese fishing nets and colonial architecture, Fort Kochi is rich in cultural heritage.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
shortDescription: A protected reserve known for elephants and boat safaris.
description: Set around the Periyar Lake, this sanctuary offers a chance to spot wildlife amid dense forests.

name: Khajuraho Temples
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage group of temples famed for intricate carvings.
description: Built by the Chandela dynasty, these temples showcase remarkable medieval Indian architecture.

name: Kanha National Park
shortDescription: One of India's largest tiger reserves and inspiration for the Jungle Book.
description: Sprawling meadows and sal forests make Kanha a prime destination for tiger sightings.

name: Sanchi Stupa
shortDescription: An ancient Buddhist monument commissioned by Emperor Ashoka.
description: This UNESCO site features some of the oldest stone structures in India.

name: Bhimbetka Caves
shortDescription: Rock shelters with prehistoric cave paintings.
description: These UNESCO listed caves offer a rare glimpse into early human life in the Indian subcontinent.

name: Gateway of India
shortDescription: Historic arch overlooking the Arabian Sea in Mumbai.
description: Built in 1924, the Gateway of India is one of Mumbai's most iconic landmarks and a popular tourist attraction.

name: Ajanta Caves
shortDescription: Ancient Buddhist rock cut caves adorned with exquisite murals.
description: This UNESCO World Heritage site dates back over 2,000 years and showcases remarkable ancient art.

name: Ellora Caves
shortDescription: A rock cut monastery complex representing Buddhist, Hindu and Jain art.
description: The Kailasa temple here is a single rock excavation and an engineering marvel of ancient India.

name: Lonavala
shortDescription: A popular hill station known for lush valleys and waterfalls.
description: Monsoon transforms Lonavala into a green paradise dotted with forts and viewpoints.

name: Loktak Lake
shortDescription: The largest freshwater lake in Northeast India, known for floating islands.
description: Its unique phumdis, or floating vegetation islands, make Loktak an ecological wonder.

name: Kangla Fort
shortDescription: A historic fort and former royal seat of the Manipuri kings.
description: The fort complex holds temples, gates and remnants of centuries old Meitei heritage.

name: Shirui Hills
shortDescription: Home to the rare Shirui Lily found nowhere else on earth.
description: The hills bloom spectacularly each summer, drawing nature lovers and trekkers.

name: Ima Keithel Market
shortDescription: A unique all women run marketplace in the heart of Imphal.
description: Run entirely by women traders, this market is a vibrant hub of local commerce and culture.

name: Living Root Bridges
shortDescription: Extraordinary bridges grown from rubber tree roots by the Khasi tribe.
description: These living structures, some centuries old, are a testament to sustainable tribal engineering.

name: Mawlynnong Village
shortDescription: Known as Asia's cleanest village, nestled amid lush greenery.
description: The village charms visitors with spotless pathways, root bridges and a lively skywalk viewpoint.

name: Umiam Lake
shortDescription: A scenic reservoir surrounded by forested hills.
description: Popular for boating and picnics, Umiam Lake offers stunning views near Shillong.

name: Dawki
shortDescription: Famous for its crystal clear river and border trade with Bangladesh.
description: The Umngot River here is renowned for its exceptional clarity, ideal for boating.

name: Reiek Hills
shortDescription: A scenic hill offering panoramic views and trekking trails.
description: The hilltop provides sweeping vistas of the surrounding valleys and traditional Mizo huts.

name: Vantawng Falls
shortDescription: Mizoram's highest waterfall, cascading through dense forest.
description: Framed by lush greenery, this multi tiered waterfall is a striking natural attraction.

name: Phawngpui Peak
shortDescription: The highest peak in Mizoram, known as the Blue Mountain.
description: Rich in rare flora and fauna, this remote peak is a haven for trekkers and nature lovers.

name: Solomon's Temple
shortDescription: A striking modern religious structure resembling ancient designs.
description: This unique landmark in Aizawl draws visitors for its architecture and hilltop setting.

name: Kohima War Cemetery
shortDescription: A poignant memorial honoring soldiers of the Battle of Kohima.
description: This peaceful, terraced cemetery commemorates a pivotal World War II battle.

name: Dzukou Valley
shortDescription: A pristine valley famed for seasonal blooms and trekking trails.
description: Rolling green hills and rare flowers make Dzukou one of the Northeast's most beautiful treks.

name: Hornbill Festival Ground
shortDescription: The venue for Nagaland's vibrant annual cultural festival.
description: Each December, this heritage village showcases tribal dances, crafts and cuisine from across Nagaland.

name: Khonoma Village
shortDescription: A model green village known for conservation and Angami culture.
description: Terraced fields and community led conservation define this pioneering eco friendly village.

name: Konark Sun Temple
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage temple shaped like a colossal chariot.
description: Dedicated to the Sun God, this 13th century temple is renowned for its intricate stone carvings.

name: Jagannath Temple
shortDescription: One of the four sacred Char Dham pilgrimage sites in India.
description: Famous for its annual Rath Yatra, this temple is a major center of devotion and tradition.

name: Chilika Lake
shortDescription: Asia's largest brackish water lagoon, rich in birdlife.
description: The lake attracts thousands of migratory birds and offers dolphin spotting boat rides.

name: Udayagiri Caves
shortDescription: Ancient rock cut caves carved for Jain ascetics.
description: These caves feature detailed carvings dating back to the 1st century BCE.

name: Golden Temple
shortDescription: The holiest Gurdwara of Sikhism, plated in gold.
description: Surrounded by a sacred pool, the Golden Temple welcomes people of all faiths with its serene langar tradition.

name: Wagah Border
shortDescription: Site of the famous daily flag lowering ceremony.
description: The spirited retreat ceremony between Indian and Pakistani forces draws large enthusiastic crowds.

name: Jallianwala Bagh
shortDescription: A memorial garden marking a pivotal moment in India's freedom struggle.
description: This historic site commemorates the tragic events of 1919 with a museum and memorial.

name: Anandpur Sahib
shortDescription: A significant Sikh pilgrimage town linked to the founding of the Khalsa.
description: The town's gurdwaras and fort hold deep historical importance in Sikh heritage.

name: Amber Fort
shortDescription: A majestic hilltop fort blending Hindu and Mughal architecture.
description: Known for its ornate mirror work and courtyards, Amber Fort is one of Rajasthan's grandest monuments.

name: Hawa Mahal
shortDescription: An iconic pink sandstone palace with a honeycomb facade.
description: Built for royal women to observe street life, this five story palace is a symbol of Jaipur.

name: Lake Pichola
shortDescription: A scenic artificial lake surrounded by palaces and hills.
description: Boat rides on Pichola offer stunning views of the City Palace and Lake Palace.

name: Jaisalmer Fort
shortDescription: A living fort rising from the Thar Desert's golden sands.
description: Unique for housing residents within its walls, this fort is often called the Golden Fort.

name: Tsomgo Lake
shortDescription: A glacial lake set amid snow capped mountains.
description: This sacred high altitude lake changes color with the seasons and is a popular day trip from Gangtok.

name: Nathula Pass
shortDescription: A historic Himalayan pass on the India China border.
description: Once part of the ancient Silk Route, Nathula offers dramatic mountain views at high altitude.

name: Gangtok
shortDescription: Sikkim's capital, known for monasteries and mountain views.
description: The city blends Buddhist culture with modern charm against a backdrop of the Kanchenjunga range.

name: Yumthang Valley
shortDescription: A stunning valley of flowers surrounded by snow peaks.
description: Known as the Valley of Flowers of Sikkim, it blooms vividly with rhododendrons each spring.

name: Meenakshi Temple
shortDescription: A stunning temple complex famed for its towering gopurams.
description: Dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi, this temple is renowned for its vibrant sculptures and architecture.

name: Marina Beach
shortDescription: One of the longest urban beaches in the world.
description: This bustling promenade along the Bay of Bengal is a favorite spot for locals and visitors alike.

name: Ooty
shortDescription: A charming hill station known as the Queen of Hill Stations.
description: Tea gardens, botanical gardens and a toy train make Ooty a beloved Nilgiri retreat.

name: Mahabalipuram
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage site of ancient rock cut temples.
description: Famous for its Shore Temple and monolithic rathas, this coastal town reflects Pallava artistry.

name: Charminar
shortDescription: An iconic 16th century monument with four grand minarets.
description: This historic landmark stands at the heart of Hyderabad's bustling old city markets.

name: Golconda Fort
shortDescription: A massive fort famed for its acoustics and diamond trade history.
description: Once home to the legendary Koh i Noor diamond, this fort showcases remarkable engineering.

name: Ramoji Film City
shortDescription: One of the world's largest integrated film studio complexes.
description: This sprawling attraction offers film sets, gardens and entertainment for visitors of all ages.

name: Hussain Sagar Lake
shortDescription: A heart shaped lake featuring a giant Buddha statue.
description: This iconic lake connects the twin cities of Hyderabad and Secunderabad with scenic promenades.

name: Ujjayanta Palace
shortDescription: A grand former royal palace now housing a state museum.
description: This elegant palace showcases Tripura's royal history amid beautifully landscaped gardens.

name: Neermahal
shortDescription: A stunning lake palace built as a royal summer retreat.
description: Situated amid the Rudrasagar Lake, this palace blends Hindu and Mughal architectural styles.

name: Unakoti
shortDescription: An ancient site of rock cut sculptures and carvings.
description: Legends surround these giant bas relief carvings hidden within the forested hills.

name: Jampui Hills
shortDescription: Tripura's highest hill range known for orange orchards.
description: The hills offer cool climate, scenic viewpoints and vibrant orange plantations.

name: Taj Mahal
shortDescription: The iconic ivory white marble mausoleum and UNESCO World Heritage site.
description: Built by Emperor Shah Jahan, the Taj Mahal stands as an eternal symbol of love and Mughal artistry.

name: Varanasi Ghats
shortDescription: Sacred riverfront steps along the Ganges known for spiritual rituals.
description: The ghats of Varanasi offer a profound glimpse into ancient rituals and daily life along the river.

name: Fatehpur Sikri
shortDescription: A UNESCO listed Mughal city built by Emperor Akbar.
description: This well preserved red sandstone complex showcases the grandeur of Mughal urban planning.

name: Agra Fort
shortDescription: A formidable red sandstone fort and former Mughal residence.
description: This UNESCO World Heritage fort offers a glimpse into the opulence of Mughal era architecture.

name: Rishikesh
shortDescription: The Yoga Capital of the World, set along the Ganges.
description: Known for ashrams, river rafting and the iconic Laxman Jhula, Rishikesh draws spiritual seekers.

name: Nainital
shortDescription: A picturesque lake town nestled in the Kumaon hills.
description: Centered around the scenic Naini Lake, this hill station is a favorite for boating and mountain views.

name: Valley of Flowers
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage national park bursting with alpine blooms.
description: This high altitude valley transforms into a vibrant carpet of wildflowers each monsoon.

name: Kedarnath
shortDescription: A revered Himalayan temple dedicated to Lord Shiva.
description: Part of the sacred Char Dham circuit, Kedarnath sits amid stunning snow clad peaks.

name: Victoria Memorial
shortDescription: A grand white marble monument dedicated to Queen Victoria.
description: This iconic landmark now houses a museum showcasing colonial era art and history.

name: Darjeeling
shortDescription: A famous hill station known for tea gardens and Himalayan views.
description: Home to the Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, this hill town offers stunning views of Kanchenjunga.

name: Sundarbans National Park
shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage mangrove forest and tiger reserve.
description: This vast delta ecosystem is the only mangrove habitat for the Royal Bengal Tiger.

name: Howrah Bridge
shortDescription: An iconic cantilever bridge spanning the Hooghly River.
description: One of the busiest bridges in the world, it stands as an enduring symbol of Kolkata.
